title: Hymenophyllum armstrongii
taxonomy: Hymenophyllum armstrongii is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae, commonly known as the filmy ferns. It belongs to the phylum Pteridophyta, which includes all vascular plants that reproduce via spores. The genus Hymenophyllum is a group of small to medium-sized ferns that are characterized by their delicate, lacy fronds. The Hymenophyllaceae family is a small group of ferns that are found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships between different species of Hymenophyllum armstrongii and other ferns.

intro: Hymenophyllum armstrongii, also known as Armstrong's filmy fern, is a species of fern native to New Zealand. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making it beneficial for treating various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of H. armstrongii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
